name: Summer Fruit Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups Mixed dried fruit (e.g., apricots, peaches, plums, nectarines); chopped fresh or dried
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9x13-inch baking pan with butter or cooking spray.
2. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon.
3. In another bowl, beat the eggs, buttermilk, cocoa powder, oil, and vanilla extract until well combined.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, stirring until just blended. Be careful not to overmix; if the batter becomes too thick, add a little more buttermilk as needed.
5. Stir in the chopped dried fruit, walnuts, flaked coconut, and raisins.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and spread evenly.
7. Bake for 45 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. For the glaze: In a saucepan over medium heat, combine sugar, buttermilk, baking soda, vanilla extract, and corn syrup. Stir constantly until the mixture boils for about 5 minutes.
9. Remove from heat and stir in the remaining vanilla extract.
10. Allow the cake to cool completely before serving.
11. Once cooled, drizzle the glaze over the top of the cake, ensuring it is evenly coated. Let the glaze set before serving.
